name: Somnus
description: Somnus, the Roman god of sleep, is a personification of slumber and tranquility. He is the son of Nox (Night) and Erebus (Darkness), and the brother of Mors (Death). Somnus dwells in a dark and silent cave, a realm beyond the sun's reach, where the river Lethe flows, offering forgetfulness. His abode is adorned with poppies and other sleep-inducing plants, and his bed is made of ebony. Somnus possesses the power to lull gods and mortals alike into a state of rest, often depicted as a gentle and benevolent figure. He is usually portrayed as a winged youth, carrying a horn filled with sleep-inducing dew or a poppy stalk. His role is essential in the natural order, providing respite and rejuvenation to the world. While generally benevolent, Somnus's power can be manipulated, as seen in stories where he is tasked with deceiving gods on behalf of others. He is a necessary part of the cycle of existence, offering a temporary escape from the burdens of reality. His influence extends to dreams, which are considered to be messages or visions from the gods or from the depths of the subconscious. He is a powerful, yet often overlooked, deity in the Roman pantheon, whose presence is felt in every instance of sleep and dreams.
subtitle: The God of Sleep and Dreams

keyMyths.title: Juno and Somnus
keyMyths.description: Juno asks Somnus to lull Jupiter to sleep so she can aid the Greeks in the Trojan War. He initially refuses out of fear of Jupiter's wrath but eventually agrees in exchange for a hand in marriage to Pasithea.

keyMyths.title: Alcyone and Ceyx
keyMyths.description: Somnus sends a dream apparition of Ceyx to Alcyone to inform her of his death at sea. This act demonstrates Somnus's ability to communicate through dreams.

keyMyths.title: Somnus's Cave
keyMyths.description: The description of Somnus's cave, a place of perpetual darkness and tranquility, emphasizes his domain and the atmosphere of sleep and dreams.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: A collection of mythological tales, including stories featuring Somnus.

sources.author: Hyginus
